title: Expected years of schooling, males vs. females
subtitle: The number of years a child of school entrance age can expect to receive if the current age-specific enrollment rates persist throughout the child’s years of schooling.
caption:
Expected years of schooling, males vs. females

The number of years a child of school entrance age can expect to receive if the current age-specific enrollment rates persist throughout the child’s years of schooling.

This scatter plot compares expected years of schooling for boys versus girls in 2023, with the horizontal axis representing girls and the vertical axis representing boys. A diagonal line marks the point of parity where both genders have equal expected schooling. Data points are color-coded by continent: North America (orange), South America (brown), Africa (purple), Europe (blue), Asia (teal), and Oceania (green), with circle size indicating population. Countries in the upper-right quadrant, such as Australia, Turkey, Ireland, and the United Kingdom, show high values for both genders, generally exceeding 19 years for girls and 20 years for boys. In the middle range, nations like China, India, Mexico, and Bangladesh cluster between 12 and 16 years for both axes, sitting close to the parity line. Several countries in Africa and Asia, including Afghanistan, Pakistan, Syria, and Senegal, appear in the lower-left section with values below 10 years for both genders. Most labeled points lie near the diagonal, suggesting similar expectations for males and females within those specific nations, though slight deviations exist where one gender’s value is marginally higher than the other. The distribution spans from approximately 5 years to over 20 years across the dataset.
